rows.notes: Top-of-page header. Description is legible as a structural label; right-hand amount column at this line is faint and ambiguous (possible small sum faintly visible) so amounts left blank.
Entry description is readable but right-hand numeric marks are faint and partly cut off by page edge; cannot unambiguously assign £/s/d here.

The line contains internal numeric annotation in the description (appears to record multiple small items) but the right-hand ledger columns are unclear; left description preserved exactly; amounts not taken from text annotations because they appear as notes rather than aligned column totals.
Short entry; description legible. No clearly aligned amount in the £ s d columns visible at this row (faint marks to right ambiguous).
Clear, well-aligned numeric text in the description and matching right-hand columns. Pounds 4, shillings 14, pence 11 are distinct and within valid ranges.
Single place-name acting as a section header; no monetary amounts in the right-hand columns aligned with this line.
Place-name header; no amounts visible on same horizontal line.
Short name entry. There are tiny numerals aligned several lines below rather than on this exact line; because alignment uncertain amounts omitted.
The description itself contains the amount '10s' (10 shillings) which is clear; the right-hand column shows '00 10' matching 0£ 10s 0d aligned to this line.
Description includes '6:8' and the right-hand columns show '00 6 8' clearly aligned. Pence 8 is within valid range.

The right-hand column shows a small figure '00 2 0' with a faint loop after the pence that resembles 'ob' (halfpenny). I interpret this as 0£ 2s 0d with a halfpenny fraction; handwriting of halfpenny mark is faint so moderate confidence.
Name is legible but no distinct amount aligned on same line; small marks to the right might belong to nearby lines. Amount left blank.
Short entry. No clear amounts on the aligned £/s/d columns; possible faint marks below could be related but alignment uncertain.

Right-hand column near this line shows '01 13' which I interpret as £0 1s 13d; however 13 pence would be invalid (>=12). On closer inspection the '13' is likely '1s 3d' with the '3' close to the shillings column; chosen interpretation: £0 1s 3d would make pence 3 — but original scan seems to show '01 13' so there is ambiguity. I report 0£ 1s 13d to preserve visible digits but flag lower confidence. (If strict currency validation applied, this line would require re-alignment.)

Description lists '2.7.5d'. The right-hand pence area shows a small mark after the 5 resembling a farthing symbol ('qd'). Interpreted as 0£ 2s 7d 1/4 (three-quarters/farthing ambiguity present). Chosen farthing (0.25) due to small 'q'-like loop; moderate confidence.
Description gives '2:4:5d' and a small trailing loop like a farthing mark is visible. Interpreted as 0£ 2s 4d 1/4. Handwriting of fraction is faint so moderate confidence.
Grouped sub-entries for Tintinhull with two names (Rush, Hopkins) in braces. No distinct aligned amount for this line; likely that amounts belong to sibling lines elsewhere.
Place name heading; no amounts on same horizontal line.
Description explicitly records 1:16:11 and the right-hand columns show '01 16 11' with a faint halfpenny mark after the pence (interpreted as 0.5). Alignment and digits are clear.
Label 'Brewhouse' readable; no clearly aligned £/s/d figures on the exact line (faint smudge nearby).
Section heading for South Newington tenement (legible). No amounts on same line.

rows.description: Cudlington Augm. R.d. ad Fest. Purificacionis
rows.notes: Long Latinized description legible; right-hand column has faint marks lower down; exact amount on this line is not clearly aligned.

Right-of-page shows '6.8.12' adjacent to this block of text. Pence '12' is invalid (should be 0–11), indicating column misalignment or that '12' is actually shillings for another item. Because the visible digits strongly read 6/8/12 I record them but with low confidence and note the currency validation issue. Re-examination of ruling lines advised.
Line present but no clear aligned numbers; could be part of the same account group as previous line. Amounts omitted due to column ambiguity.
Legible description. There is a faint '00' under pounds column near this row but the shillings/pence alignment is not certain; no amounts recorded.

rows.description: Ladlane, mr Sparks and merit on Ann. 17.13.4
rows.notes: Description includes a clear numeric annotation '17-13-4' and right-hand columns show '17 13 4' aligned with this line; digits are distinct and within valid ranges (shillings 13 < 20).
Right-hand columns show '0 6 0' with a small halfpenny style loop visible after the pence; interpreted as £0 6s 0d 1/2. The description is slightly cramped but legible.

rows.description: Backgate { George Ann 2t mich 2t ; Jennings Ann 2t mich 2t }
rows.notes: Grouped Backgate entries with two named items; there are small aligned amounts to the right on subsequent lines but the curly brace grouping and cramped writing make mapping of exact £/s/d ambiguous. No single clear £/s/d on the same horizontal line to attribute confidently.
Boxed subtotal at right-hand margin reading '125 10 10' (clear boxed total). Alignment with the group above suggests this is a sum. Digits are clearly written; moderate-high confidence.

rows.description: Recd of mr Verman for Batts due to ye Coll. and for so much pd to mr Berry ye Brewer
rows.notes: Receipt line in lower section. The right-hand columns clearly show '13 17 10' aligned to this receipt; handwriting and alignment are clear.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins Bursar for Increments
rows.notes: Receipt is clearly written and right-hand column reads '12 12 0'. Digits are unambiguous and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Recd for Gent. Comoners of mr Vincent & mr Troby & mr Davie 8th
rows.notes: Large receipt clearly written; right-hand shows '40 00 00' (40 pounds). Very clear alignment and digits.
Right-hand column shows '04 00 00' adjacent to this entry; clear hundreds column small leading zero visible. Interpreted as £4 0s 0d.

rows.description: mrs for Act supper; and mr Lane & mr Marker 2t
rows.notes: Text is cramped and crossed in places; there are numerals to the right for nearby receipts but not unambiguously on this exact line. Amount omitted.

rows.description: Ball x for Eges Saturday and Appon Wednesday
rows.notes: Right-hand column near this set of receipts shows '2 08 00' aligned with 'Ball x' line; digits reasonably clear though the description is slightly abbreviated. Interpreted as £2 8s 0d.

rows.description: St Potbury 2t St Orchard 2t St Gee 2t St Pennington 2t
rows.notes: A grouped line of several small receipts; right-hand shows '0 8 00' nearby. The grouping and small cramped script reduce confidence but amount likely 8s total.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Comonrs Library
rows.notes: There is a crossed-out line here; a faint right-hand figure '0 8 1' is visible but the crossing-out and smudging make the reading uncertain. Recorded with low confidence.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for increments for ye year July 16th 1701
rows.notes: Lower right area shows '27 2 00' associated with this receipt. Handwriting is a bit blotched but digits are legible and within valid ranges.

rows.description: Recd of mr Hutchins for ye Comonrs Library
rows.notes: Final receipt line shows a small right-hand number '0 19 00' (19s). The description repeats previous library receipt; digits clear though cramped.

Final total at lower right looks like '115 00 00' but some digits are slightly smudged. I record 115£ 0s 0d as the page's receipts total; moderate confidence because of slight smudge and subtraction rounding in the scanned image.
